idea.title: Bumper Ball
idea.hook: Flick a bumper-car puck like a pool cue to knock the ball into the goal — within a tight, shareable shot budget.
idea.hook_ru: Щёлкни шайбу-бампер как кием и загони шар в ворота — в рамках жёсткого лимита ударов.
idea.prototype: Top-down physics puck game. Drag back anywhere to aim + set power (pool-cue / slingshot style) with a live aim line + power gauge; release to fire the puck. The puck collides with the ball, springy bumpers and bouncy walls with rolling friction so everything settles. GOAL: knock the silver ball into the glowing goal zone. Non-timer pressure is a SHOT BUDGET (5 shots/level) — run out before the ball is in the goal = lose the level. Level-based progression across 6 hand-built layouts that add static bumpers, bouncy walls, and hazard pits that reset the ball to its spawn (positional progress lost, no shot refunded).
idea.why_novel: a bumper-car puck flicked like a cue with a shot budget — the slingshot/flick genre on these portals (Angry-Birds clones, golf games) aims a single projectile at static targets; Bumper Ball makes the player flick a heavy PUCK that must then carom into a SECOND free body (the ball) and shepherd IT into the goal, a two-body knock-on physics puzzle constrained by a per-level shot economy rather than a timer.
idea.lose_condition: gs='GAMEOVER' when shotsLeft<=0 and the ball is not inside the goal zone after all bodies come to rest (allAtRest()).

sources.name: Asphalt Racing
sources.note: CTR reference — saturated racing-vehicle cover composition.

synthesizer_brief: Daily TRANSFER BET (signal) slot — borrow a high-CTR vehicle_crash cover archetype from the racing_vehicle family and attach it to a fresh, honest flick-physics mechanic. The puck is a bumper-car disc; the player drags back anywhere on screen (pool-cue / slingshot) to aim + set power, releases to fire, and the puck caroms off springy bumpers and bouncy walls to knock a free silver ball into a glowing goal. The only enemy is a SHOT BUDGET (5/level), never a timer; hazard pits add a second pressure axis by resetting the ball without refunding a shot. 6 hand-built levels escalate from a clean straight shot to a bumper-and-pit gauntlet. Readability is the priority: goal obvious in 5s ('get the ball in the goal'), one-thumb drag-to-aim that works anywhere on screen, an always-visible shot-pip HUD, and a live aim line + power gauge while dragging. Cover borrows the impossible-angle / impact-moment composition from the vehicle_crash references but stays honest to the puck-vs-ball mechanic (glossy puck mid-impact, ball rocketing toward a glowing goal, motion lines, saturated arena floor, no text).
